title: Voyria aphylla
intro: Voyria aphylla, also known as the leafless Voyria, is a rare and unusual plant species belonging to the Gentianaceae family. Traditionally, Voyria aphylla has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Voyria aphylla include gentiopicrin, gentianine, and amarogentin, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Voyria aphylla is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory ailments.
